faqs.question: How do I know if I have a clothes moth infestation in my Inwood home?
faqs.answer: Signs of clothes moth infestation include small irregular holes in wool, silk, or cashmere clothing, silken webbing in closets, small beige or gold-colored moths flying near wardrobes, and larvae casings in fabric folds. You may also notice damage to upholstered furniture, carpets, or stored blankets. If you spot any of these signs in your Inwood home, contact us immediately for an inspection.

faqs.answer: Clothes moth control in Inwood typically ranges from $550 to $5,000 depending on the severity of the infestation, the size of your home, and the number of treatments required. Minor infestations in single rooms cost less, while whole-apartment treatments in larger Inwood residences require more extensive service. faqs.question: How can I prevent clothes moths from returning after treatment?
faqs.answer: Prevention includes regular vacuuming of closets and storage areas, storing woolens and natural fabrics in sealed containers, dry cleaning items before seasonal storage, and maintaining low humidity levels. We provide detailed prevention guidance after treatment and offer follow-up inspections to ensure your Inwood home remains moth-free. Cedar products and moth repellents can also help protect your clothing between professional treatments.
